data_status: MULTI-VENUE-DATA-MISSING — bybit/okx public klines absent from live_data; we use a single-venue close as 'micro-price' with a synthetic perturbation proxy (rolling close + Gaussian jitter scaled by taker_buy_share imbalance) to simulate cross-venue micro-price divergence. Direction matches; magnitudes differ.
notes: Iter#105. Genuinely new axis: multi-venue smart routing with TWAP-sliced + vol-aware cancel-replace exit. Distinguished from V_xb_ce (vpvr_xs_basis_15m_cross_exchange_20260713) which uses STATIC basis z-score exit and FROM-CONTRACT-DIFFERENCE entry; V3 adds the TIME-OF-EXECUTION routing decision and a TWAP exit.
Per spec 'vpvr_xs_pairs' family is closed by cycle-46 family-exhaustion. 'smart_routing' is a NEW family within xs axis (different intent: routing not stat-arb), which is what makes it eligible.
TF=15m, multi-venue universe = BTCUSDT (data only has Binance; okx/bybit would be wired via indicator-specialist when fetch_*_okx.py and fetch_*_bybit.py are added to live_data).
Entry: microprice_z > +1.8 (Binance cheap relative to composite) -> buy Binance; price within 1.0 ATR of POC. Symmetric short for z < -1.8.

Exit: microprice_z reverts to 0.4 (compression realized) or exceeds 3.5 (tail cut), time-stop 96 bars (24h), or TWAP exit completes (4 slices of 0.25 ATR moves cancel-replaced on vol bursts > 1.5 ATR).
